title: Prune stale immutable ConfigMaps after deployment
summary: Adds PruneImmutableConfigMaps to internal/common/config/ that lists, filters by owner+prefix, sorts by CreationTimestamp, and deletes ConfigMaps beyond the retain count. Wired into the Keystone reconcile loop after reconcileDeployment via a thin pruneStaleConfigMaps wrapper with hardcoded retain=3. 12 unit tests, 2 integration tests, and 3 controller-level tests cover all edge cases. Reference docs updated in kubernetes-packages.md and keystone-reconciler.md.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass (12 prune tests in config_test.go, 3 in reconcile_config_test.go)
Integration tests exist (2 in integration_test.go with envtest)
Edge cases covered: retain=0, fewer-than-retain, no-historical, different-owner, no-owner, mismatched-prefix, overlapping-prefix
Idempotency tested (second call produces same result)
Concurrency safety tested (NotFound on delete is silently ignored)
Current ConfigMap is never deleted even with retain=0
Retention is by CreationTimestamp not alphabetical order

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (NewGomegaWithT, fake.NewClientBuilder, client.IgnoreNotFound)
Functions do one thing (PruneImmutableConfigMaps: list+filter+sort+delete; pruneStaleConfigMaps: thin wrapper)
No code duplication — shared function in config package, thin wrapper in controller
Error wrapping with fmt.Errorf and %w for errors.Is/As compatibility
No dead code or unused imports

security_checklist.item: Owner UID matching prevents cross-CR ConfigMap deletion
No user input directly processed (baseName derived from CR name validated by Kubernetes)
client.IgnoreNotFound prevents TOCTOU race from causing errors
No secrets handled in this code path

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — list+filter+sort+delete with no unnecessary abstractions
Pruning placed after reconcileDeployment to ensure pods are on new config before old ConfigMaps are deleted
Shared library function (config.PruneImmutableConfigMaps) reusable by future operators

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code between config package and controller
No speculative features (no CRD-configurable retain count, no label-based filtering)

fail_fast_checklist.item: List errors returned immediately with context
Delete errors returned immediately with namespace/name context
Early return when candidates <= retain (no unnecessary iteration)

defensive_checklist.item: Handles ConfigMaps owned by different controllers (skipped)

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a label (e.g., forge.c5c3.io/config-base=<baseName>) to ConfigMaps created by CreateImmutableConfigMap in a future feature, allowing PruneImmutableConfigMaps to use a label selector instead of listing all ConfigMaps in the namespace. This would improve performance in namespaces with many unrelated ConfigMaps but is not needed at current scale.
